diagrams.description: Linguistic Anthropology is the study of how language shapes social life, culture, and identity. This flowchart outlines the process: it aims to understand language as a social practice and cultural resource. Core components include language and thought (linguistic relativity), language socialization, speech communities, and language ideology. These are investigated through ethnographic fieldwork and discourse analysis, guided by major theoretical frameworks (indexicality, performativity, language ideologies). The resulting insights apply to language policy, education, and cross‑cultural communication.
